member.name: T:NExpect.Assertions
member.summary: Used by NExpect to throw assertion errors

member.name: M:NExpect.Assertions.RegisterAssertionsFactory``1(System.Func{System.String,``0})
member.summary: Register your own factory for generating assertion exceptions. Threads may override this factory.
member.param: Func to invoke when NExpect needs an assertion exception

member.name: M:NExpect.Assertions.RegisterAssertionsFactory``1(System.Func{System.String,System.Exception,``0})
member.summary: Register your own global factory for generating assertion exceptions where you assertion can also take an inner exception. Threads may override this factory.

member.name: M:NExpect.Assertions.RegisterAssertionsFactoryForCurrentThread``1(System.Func{System.String,System.Exception,``0})
member.summary: Installs an assertions generator for this thread only

member.name: M:NExpect.Assertions.RemoveAssertionsFactoryForCurrentThread
member.summary: Uninstalls any assertions generator for this thread, if there is one

member.name: M:NExpect.Assertions.UseDefaultAssertionsFactory
member.summary: Resets the global factory for generating assertion exceptions to the default (ie, throws UnmetExpectationException instances)

member.name: M:NExpect.Assertions.TemporarilyUseDefaultAssertionsFactory
member.summary: Temporarily revert to using the default assertions factory (global)

member.name: M:NExpect.Assertions.TemporarilyUseDefaultAssertionsFactoryForThisThread
member.summary: Temporarily revert to UnmetExpectationExceptions, for this thread only

member.name: M:NExpect.CollectionDeepEquivalenceMatchers.To``1(NExpect.Interfaces.ICollectionDeepEquivalent{``0},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.Object[])
member.summary: Does deep-equivalence testing on two collections, ignoring complex item referencing. Two collections are deep-equivalent when their object data matches, but not necessarily in order. member.name: T:NExpect.Exceptions.NExpectEnvironment
member.summary: Easier interface for setting environment variables which NExpect will observe

member.name: T:NExpect.Exceptions.NExpectEnvironment.Variables
member.summary: These are the names of the environment variables which NExpect will observe

member.name: F:NExpect.Exceptions.NExpectEnvironment.Variables.FULL_STACK_FRAMES
member.summary: NExpect will attempt to hide stack frames which are not interesting, ie they have come from within the bowels of NExpect or some matcher. If you want all of this extra noise, set NEXPECT_FULL_STACK_FRAMES to any non-empty value

member.name: F:NExpect.Exceptions.NExpectEnvironment.Variables.DEEP_EQUALITY_IGNORES_DATE_TIME_KIND
member.summary: PeanutButter's DeepEqualityTester will, by default, test date time kinds when traversing an object hierarchy. If you don't care about mismatched DateTimeKinds on your DateTime values, then set DEEP_EQUALITY_IGNORES_DATE_TIME_KIND to any non-empty value

member.name: F:NExpect.Exceptions.NExpectEnvironment.Variables.TASK_TIMEOUT_MS
member.summary: When NExpect has to wait for the result of a task, there is likely a time after which the test should be considered failed due to timeout. If your tests are failing from timeout and you don't mind that they're taking a really long time, set TASK_TIMEOUT_MS to a number indicating the number of milliseconds to wait before considering a task timed out

member.name: F:NExpect.Exceptions.NExpectEnvironment.Variables.MAX_LINE_LENGTH
member.summary: When emitting errors, NExpect will attempt to make the error text fit appropriately on the display device. In the case of a well-behaved console, there should be a COLS environment variable -- which will be respected. For every other case, you may set MAX_LINE_LENGTH to the number of characters you'd like to max out a line at. NExpect will attempt to respect word boundaries too.

member.name: T:NExpect.Exceptions.NExpectEnvironment.Defaults
member.summary: Establishes the default values for NExpect environment variables, where appropriate

member.name: F:NExpect.Exceptions.NExpectEnvironment.Defaults.TASK_TIMEOUT_MS
member.summary: The default timeout for tasks that NExpect is waiting on: 5 seconds

member.name: F:NExpect.Exceptions.NExpectEnvironment.Defaults.MAX_LINE_LENGTH
member.summary: Default max-width for an equality failure message line. When the message would run over this length, it will be split onto multiple lines for easier reading

member.name: P:NExpect.Exceptions.NExpectEnvironment.MaxLineLength
member.summary: Determines the max line length (in number of characters) for the current display device, according to: - environment variable COLS - environment variable NEXPECT_MAX_LINE_LENGTH = fallback to 72 characters

member.name: P:NExpect.Exceptions.NExpectEnvironment.TaskTimeoutMs
member.summary: Determines the configured task timeout in milliseconds for when NExpect is waiting on a task. Defaults to 5000 (5 seconds).

member.name: P:NExpect.Exceptions.NExpectEnvironment.DeepEqualityIgnoresDateTimeKind
member.summary: Determines if Deep Equality Testing should ignore DateTimeKind values on DateTime values. Since this comes from PeanutButter, there's no NEXPECT_ prefix

member.name: P:NExpect.Exceptions.NExpectEnvironment.FullStackFrames
member.summary: Determines whether or not full stack frames should be shown for NExpect UnmetExpectations. By default, NExpect will attempt to suppress stack frames which are not interesting in the scope of your test by omitting frames which appear to come from within NExpect or a matcher. You may disable that behavior by setting FullStackFrames to true and enjoy the noise!

member.name: T:NExpect.Helpers.FuncFactory
member.summary: Helper class to memoize functions

member.name: M:NExpect.Helpers.FuncFactory.Memoize``1(System.Func{``0})
member.summary: Memoizes a func so that it's only called once

member.name: T:NExpect.Implementations.CannotBeCompared
member.summary: Defines a class which cannot be compared to any other (throws exceptions when you try). This prevents accidental usage of .Equal() instead of .Equals() in expectations.

member.name: M:NExpect.Implementations.CannotBeCompared.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Throws when invoked. Not to be used to check equality.

member.name: M:NExpect.Implementations.CannotBeCompared.GetHashCode
member.summary: Throws when invoked. Why do you want to hash an expectation, grasshopper?

member.name: T:NExpect.Implementations.ContinuationFactory
member.summary: Factory to create continuations where the continuation is an IExpectationContext<T>

member.name: P:NExpect.Implementations.ExpectationContext`1.Parent
member.summary: Parent context for this expectation

member.name: M:NExpect.Implementations.ExpectationContext`1.Negate
member.summary: Negates the current expectation

member.name: M:NExpect.Implementations.ExpectationContext`1.ResetNegation
member.summary: Resets expectation negatin

member.name: M:NExpect.Implementations.ExpectationContext`1.RunMatcher(System.Func{`0,NExpect.MatcherLogic.IMatcherResult})
member.summary: Runs an expectation by pushing it up the ancestry to be run within NExpect.

member.name: T:NExpect.Implementations.InstanceOfHelperExtensions
member.summary: Provides a helper extension to test if an expectation context is negated

member.name: M:NExpect.Implementations.InstanceOfHelperExtensions.IsNegated(NExpect.Interfaces.IExpectationContext)
member.summary: Returns the negated status for the expectation context being operated on

member.name: T:NExpect.Implementations.MessageHelpers
member.summary: Provides some helpers for formatting and creating failure messages

member.name: F:NExpect.Implementations.MessageHelpers.NULL_STRING
member.summary: Provides an easy access to a null string value - use to disambiguate calls between the version which takes a static string and the version which takes a Func<string>

member.name: F:NExpect.Implementations.MessageHelpers.NULL_GENERATOR
member.summary: Provides easy access to a null custom message generator

member.name: F:NExpect.Implementations.MessageHelpers.NULL_REPLACER
member.summary: Not to be confused with NULL, this is the string put in place of nulls within stringified messages

member.name: M:NExpect.Implementations.MessageHelpers.FinalMessageFor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Provides a final message, given a standard message and a custom message - if the custom message is null, the standard message alone is returned
member.param: Message which should always be shown at the point of failure
Optional custom message to show if not null
member.returns: Final message derived from the two parameters

member.name: M:NExpect.Implementations.MessageHelpers.FinalMessageFor(System.Func{System.String},System.Func{System.String})
member.summary: Provides a final message, given a standard message generator and a custom message generator. If the custom message generator return null or whitespace, the standard message alone is returned from the result func.

member.name: M:NExpect.Implementations.MessageHelpers.FinalMessageFor(System.String[],System.String)
member.summary: Creates a final message, given standard message parts and a custom message. When the parts, concatenated with a space, are longer than the expected max line length, they are split across lines.

member.name: M:NExpect.Implementations.MessageHelpers.FinalMessageFor(System.String[],System.Func{System.String})
member.summary: Creates a final message, given standard message parts and a custom message generator. When the parts, concatenated with a space, are longer than the expected max line length, they are split across lines.

member.name: M:NExpect.Implementations.MessageHelpers.FinalMessageFor(System.Func{System.String[]},System.Func{System.String})
member.summary: Creates a final message, given standard message parts generator and a custom message generator. When the parts, concatenated with a space, are longer than the expected max line length, they are split across lines.

member.name: M:NExpect.Implementations.MessageHelpers.AsNot(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Provides the "not" for a matcher message, based on the passed flag. When your matcher passes, you get "not ", so you can do, eg $"Expected foo {not}to be bar" When your matcher fails, you get an empty string, which also works for the example given.

member.name: M:NExpect.Implementations.MessageHelpers.Quote``1(``0)
member.summary: Quotes a string or object, as required. Only non-null strings get quotes.
member.returns: String representation of object

member.summary: Returns a collection as a comma-separated list

member.summary: Returns string with up to 10 elements from a collection with ellipsis if required

member.name: P:NExpect.MatcherLogic.IMatcherResult.Message
member.summary: Message to display. In the case when your matcher passes, put in the message you would display if your result were negated, for example, if testing equality to 1, when Passed is false, one would return: "expected value to equal 1" and one would then say, when Passed is true: "expected value not to equal 1"

member.name: M:NExpect.MatcherLogic.MatcherHelperExtensionMethods.GetExpectedCount``1(NExpect.Interfaces.ICountMatch)
member.summary: Acting on a continuation, will return the ExpectedCount for that continuation, either directly by casting to the internal concrete implementation, or, if you have your own implementation, looking, via reflection for the ExpectedCount property as an integer. Use like: var expected = {continuation}.GetExpectedCount();

member.summary: Acting on a continuation, will return the Method for that continuation, either directly by casting to the internal concrete implementation, or, if you have your own implementation, looking, via reflection for the Method property as an integer. Use like: var method = {continuation}.GetCountMatchMethod();

member.summary: Constructor with static message Consider rather using the constructor with the Func<string> since that message will only be evaluated late, once, meaning that if there is any costly work to be done (eg deep .Stringify()) calls, then that work will only be done when the message is read to display to the user, instead of every time the assertion is made.

member.summary: Constructs a new AutoLocker, immediately locking the provided Mutex
member.exception: Thrown if the provided Mutex is null

member.name: T:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.AutoResetter
member.summary: Provides a mechanism to run code on construction and disposal, irrespective of exception handling Use this, for example, to set up and tear down state required for a test -- your constructionAction is called immediately upon construction and the using() pattern guarantees that your disposalAction is called at disposal, even if your test fails.

member.name: M:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.AutoResetter.#ctor(System.Action,System.Action)
member.summary: Constructs a new AutoResetter and immediately runs the constructionAction
member.param: Action to run at construction time
Action to run at disposal time

member.name: T:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.DeepEqualityTester
member.summary: Provides a mechanism to test deep-equality of two objects with an optional list of properties to ignore by name. Deep equality testing bypasses reference-checking of container objects and compares primitive propertyt values. Use this to test whether or not two objects essentially contain the same data. More conveniently, use the following extension methods: - DeepEquals -> performs default deep equality testing - DeepSubEquals -> tests if one object matches another, for all the properties that the first has in common with the second - DeepIntersectionEquals -> tests deep equality only on properties which can be matched by name and type

member.name: T:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.DeepEqualityTester.EnumComparisonStrategies
member.summary: Describes available methods for comparing enum values

member.name: F:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.DeepEqualityTester.EnumComparisonStrategies.ByName
member.summary: Compare enum values by name (default)

member.name: F:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.DeepEqualityTester.EnumComparisonStrategies.ByObjectEquals
member.summary: Compare enum values by object equality (.Equals())

member.name: F:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.DeepEqualityTester.EnumComparisonStrategies.ByIntegerValue
member.summary: Compare enum values by integer value

member.name: P:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.DeepEqualityTester.RecordErrors
member.summary: Toggle whether or not to record equality errors

member.name: P:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.DeepEqualityTester.FailOnMissingProperties
member.summary: Toggle whether or not equality testing fails when properties found on the first object are not found on the corresponding other object

member.name: P:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.DeepEqualityTester.OnlyTestIntersectingProperties
member.summary: Toggle whether or not to only test properties found on both objects

member.name: P:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.DeepEqualityTester.Errors
member.summary: Provides a list of errors for diagnosing inequality, if RecordErrors has been set to true

member.name: P:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.DeepEqualityTester.IncludeFields
member.summary: Flag: include fields in deep equality testing (false by default)

member.name: P:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.DeepEqualityTester.OnlyCompareShape
member.summary: Toggle only testing the shape of the objects provided.

member.name: P:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.DeepEqualityTester.VerbosePropertyMismatchErrors
member.summary: Include full object dumps when storing errors about property mismatches

member.name: P:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.DeepEqualityTester.EnumComparisonStrategy
member.summary: When comparing enum values, forget their type and only compare their integer values

member.summary: Constructs a new DeepEqualityTester for a source object and compare object with an optional params array of properties to ignore by name, all the way down
member.param: Params array of properties to ignore by name

member.name: M:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.DeepEqualityTester.AreDeepEqual
member.summary: Calculates if the two objects provided during construction are DeepEqual according to the properties set. Will always re-calculate, so if one of the provided objects changes, this will always return the current value.
member.returns: True if the two objects are found to match; false otherwise.

member.summary: Adds a custom comparer for the type T

member.summary: Adds a custom comparer to use for the specified type. Custom comparers must implement IComparer<T> where T becomes the type selection to use for when the comparer is invoked

member.name: M:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.ObjectExtensions.DeepEquals(System.Object,System.Object,System.String[])
member.summary: Runs a deep equality test between two objects, glossing over reference differences between class-types and comparing only primitive types. Use this when you'd like to essentially test whether the data in one object hierachy matches that of another
member.param: Object which is the source of truth
Params array of properties to ignore by name

member.name: M:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.ObjectExtensions.ShapeEquals(System.Object,System.Object,System.String[])
member.summary: Tests if two objects have the same "shape" -- basically Deep Equality testing without actually testing final property values.
member.returns: True if the "shapes" are the same, false otherwise

member.name: M:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.ObjectExtensions.ShapeSubEquals(System.Object,System.Object,System.String[])
member.summary: Tests if a second object has at least the "shape" of a primary one. Basically a DeepSubEquals without testing final property values. without actually testing final property values.
member.returns: True if the comparison object "contains the shape" of the source object, false otherwise

member.name: M:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.ObjectExtensions.DeepEquals(System.Object,System.Object,Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.ObjectComparisons,System.String[])
member.summary: Runs a deep equality test between two objects, glossing over reference differences between class-types and comparing only primitive types. Use this when you'd like to essentially test whether the data in one object hierachy matches that of another
member.param: Object which is the source of truth
Params array of properties to ignore by name

member.name: M:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.ObjectExtensions.DeepEquals(System.Object,System.Object,System.Action{System.String},System.String[])
member.summary: Runs a deep equality test between two objects, ignoring reference differences wherever possible and logging failures with the provided action. Properties can be explided by name with the ignorePropertiesByName params

member.name: M:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.ObjectExtensions.DeepSubEquals(System.Object,System.Object,System.String[])
member.summary: Runs a deep equality test between two objects, using the properties on objSource (and children) as the set of properties to match on
member.param: Source object to perform comparison against
Optional params array of properties to ignore by name
member.returns: True if relevant properties are found and match; false otherwise

member.name: M:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.ObjectExtensions.DeepIntersectionEquals(System.Object,System.Object,System.String[])
member.summary: Runs a deep equality test between two objects, using the properties common to both sides of the comparison to match on.
member.param: Source object to perform comparison against
Optional params array of properties to ignore by name
member.returns: True if relevant properties are found and match; false otherwise. If no common properties are found, returns false; caveat: performing this comparison on two vanilla Object() instances will return true.

member.name: T:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.MetadataExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods to set and retrieve metadata on any object. Under the hood, these methods use a ConditionalWeakTable to store your metadata, so the metadata is garbage-collected when your managed objects are garbage-collected.

member.name: M:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.MetadataExtensions.SetMetadata(System.Object,System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Sets an arbitrary piece of metadata on a managed object. This metadata has the same lifetime as your object, meaning it will be garbage-collected when your object is garbage-collected, assuming nothing else is referencing it.
member.param: Name of the metadata item to set

member.name: M:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.MetadataExtensions.GetMetadata``1(System.Object,System.String)
member.summary: Attempts to retrieve a piece of metadata for an object. When the metadata key for the object is unknown, returns the default value for the type requested, eg 0 for ints, null for strings and objects. Note that if metadata exists for the requested key but not for the type requested, a type-casting exception will be thrown.

member.name: M:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.MetadataExtensions.GetMetadata``1(System.Object,System.String,``0)
member.summary: Attempts to retrieve a piece of metadata for an object. When the metadata key for the object is unknown, returns the default value for the type requested, eg 0 for ints, null for strings and objects. Note that if metadata exists for the requested key but not for the type requested, a type-casting exception will be thrown. This overload allows specifying a default value.

member.summary: Tests if an object has any metadata stored against it at all - will always return false for a null object

member.name: M:Imported.PeanutButter.Utils.MetadataExtensions.HasMetadata(System.Object,System.String)
member.summary: Tests if an object has metadata with the provided key - will always return false for a null object
